rules.rule: CROSS-SESSION: Returns declining 295%->138%->101%->62% - brain learning shorter holds, NOT strategy failure
CROSS-SESSION: Diversification improving - top market 85%->44%->24%->31% - healthy risk distribution
CROSS-SESSION: 280+ paper trades, 2 adverse fills total - fill sim is 3-5x too optimistic, multiply paper returns by 0.25 for live estimate
CROSS-SESSION: Israeli parliament = most consistent earner across all sessions (100% WR, 33+ trades, $1.07 avg) - STAR confirmed
CROSS-SESSION: USDT $200B = second most reliable (98% WR, $1.44 avg) - STAR confirmed
CROSS-SESSION: OpenAI hardware = steady earner (100% WR, $0.81 avg, short holds) - STAR confirmed
LIVE RECOMMENDATION: Go live with $100, one_side, per-order $5, max 3 concurrent - paper testing exhausted
LIVE RECOMMENDATION: Expected live metrics - 60-75% WR, +2% to -5% per session, 15-25% adverse fills, 5-15% drawdown
LIVE RECOMMENDATION: 2-week trial minimum before evaluating - track fill rates, adverse %, gas costs vs paper predictions
SENTIMENT: Module underperforms in paper mode - regular one_side flow captures same markets more efficiently. Consider disabling for live to reduce noise.
HOLD TIME: 0-30s and 30-60s hold times have 100% WR across all sessions. 300s+ hold times have 73% WR. Target <60s holds.

lessons: 85% of profits from one market (Fed 5.5%) - extreme concentration risk
Paper fill simulator v9 is deterministic but still more favorable than live
Token inventory bug: bot opens unintended SHORTs when selling more tokens than owned
Kelly fraction at 25% is too aggressive for prediction markets
Flow pull on 50-93c momentum surges is correct - these are significant on PM
Brain correctly identified STAR markets with 100% WR
Tight spread markets (0.06-0.10) have 100% WR - fast fills
Low price range (5-20c) has highest PnL - spread capture is largest here
bugs_found: Token inventory: SELL opens SHORT when bot does not own enough tokens
Stripe IPO: SHORT 115 @ 0.144, timeout exit lost -8.04
USDT market cap: SHORT 5 shares on SELL side
OpenAI: SHORT at 0.414 on SELL side
recommendations: Reduce Kelly to 5-10%
Fix token inventory tracking - prevent unintended SHORTs
Cap per-market exposure at 30% of total PnL
Add gas cost simulation (0.5c per trade)
Increase paper tax to 0.5c for more realistic slippage
Test one_side strategy to avoid token inventory issues
Run during peak hours (14-22 UTC) for better fills

lessons: one_side strategy avoids token inventory bugs seen in both_sides (no unintended SHORTs)
99% WR is unrealistic - paper fill simulator produces 0 adverse fills in 83 trades
Stripe IPO carried 44% of total PnL - single market dependency is dangerous
Top 3 markets = 70% of PnL - concentration risk is extreme
Adverse selection simulator needs tightening: 0/83 adverse fills is impossible in live
Sentiment trading triggered 5 initial orders from Iran ceasefire news - working correctly
Dynamic stops fired once, correctly protected (still profited +$0.09 on stop hit)
Flow pulls fired 10 times on momentum surges - good risk management
Timeout exits (3) were all profitable - brain hold time tuning works
Realistic live expectations: 2-5% daily return, 60-75% WR, regular small losses
Mid-relative BUY placement (1-3c below mid) outperformed best-bid placement
Post-only orders working as intended - never crossed the spread
Brain learned STAR markets: waymo, republicans, israel-gaza-offensive
bugs_found: Fill simulator adverse selection: 0 adverse fills in 83 trades is unrealistic
Paper mode 100% WR (later 99%) suggests fill model is too lenient
Stripe IPO market: bought 250 shares @ 0.106, sold @ 0.170 - unrealistic fill on large order
No drawdown in 30 min of active trading is a red flag for paper realism
recommendations: Tighten FillSimulator adverse selection - target 10-20% adverse fill rate
Add paper tax of 0.5-1c per fill to simulate slippage
Cap per-market PnL contribution at 30% to force diversification
Add market liquidity cap: never order > 2% of book depth
Calibrate fill simulator against actual Polymarket trade data
Before live: run 1-week paper session and compare fill prices
Consider reducing position sizes by 50% for first live week

session_analyses.termination: SIGTERM - process killed externally, 2 positions still open
lessons: Equity accelerated in final 10 min ($199->$264 in 4 min) - bot increased sizing as Kelly confidence grew on 100% WR
Stripe IPO dominated again with 64 round-trip scalps at $0.11-$0.13 range - same pattern as session 6
100% WR across 88 trades confirms fill simulator is still too lenient - no adverse fills generated
Brain correctly promoted markets to STAR: nhl-hart-mcdavid, trump-pardon-bannon
Dynamic stops working: set on every entry, trailing up on winners, never triggered (no losses)
Post-only orders filling consistently at favorable prices - unrealistic for live thin books
one_side strategy performing well - no token inventory issues, clean entries/exits
Sentiment and arbitrage modules active but not contributing significant edge in this session
ML brain hit 294 trades in memory - learning is accumulating but inflated by paper fills
bugs_found: Fill simulator adverse selection: 0/88 adverse fills is statistically impossible in real markets
Stripe IPO bought 300 shares @ $0.110 and sold @ $0.130 repeatedly - live liquidity can't support this
Bankroll.json not updated on SIGTERM kill - peak equity $263.62 was not persisted
Bot was killed at 03:48 but restarted briefly at 03:55 for one more fill - unclear restart mechanism
No drawdown in 30 min with 88 trades is a definitive red flag for paper realism
recommendations: CRITICAL: Tighten FillSimulator adverse selection rate to 10-20% before any live deployment
Add per-fill slippage tax of 0.5-1c to simulate real market friction
Cap per-market PnL contribution at 30% to enforce diversification and reduce single-market dependency
Add liquidity guard: never place orders > 2% of displayed book depth
Calibrate paper fills against real Polymarket execution data (run parallel paper+live on small size)
Fix bankroll persistence: save state on SIGTERM/SIGINT via signal handler
Reduce Kelly fraction ceiling from 25% to 10% for first live sessions
Add random partial fills (20-40% of orders) to simulate queue position reality
Before live: run 5+ paper sessions and verify WR drops to 65-80% range with adverse fills enabled
Consider reducing starting position sizes by 75% for first live week

session_analyses.termination: timeout - 30 min kill signal, no open positions at exit
lessons: Return declining across sessions: 295% -> 138% -> 101% -> 62% - brain learning shorter hold times = smaller per-trade profits
Diversification improving: top market dropped from 85% (#5) to 31% (#10) - healthy trend
Equity curve acceleration visible: first half +$23/12min, second half +$38/17min - compounding effect of bankroll scaling
Israeli parliament is the most consistent earner across 4 sessions: +$19.34 (#10), +$17.44 (#9), +$12.36 (#6)
USDT market cap is second most reliable: +$14.39 (#10), +$18.78 (#9) - 15c price with ~15c spread = near 100% capture
NHL Hart Trophy remains explosive: +$13.30 in 9 trades ($1.48 avg) - extreme volatility on thin books
OpenAI hardware is the steady earner: +$11.30 in 14 trades, 100% WR, consistent 7-8c spread capture
Sentiment module underperformed: 4 signals fired at T+0, placed 5 buys, but regular one_side flow captured same markets more efficiently
0 adverse fills in 4 consecutive sessions (280+ trades total, only 2 adverse ever) - fill simulator is 3-5x too optimistic
0 drawdown in 4 consecutive sessions - even best live scalpers see 3-8% drawdowns
Paper-mode multiplier estimated at 3-5x: divide paper returns by 4 for realistic live expectations
Growth rate was remarkably linear at ~$2.05/minute - no blowup spikes, steady accumulation
Market pruning doing heavy lifting: 20+ dead markets removed across session - keeps bot focused on active books
Brain has 598 trades in memory now - learning is accumulating but all paper-mode inflated
bugs_found: Fill simulator still producing 0 adverse fills - same issue for 4th consecutive session
0% drawdown impossible in real market making with 63 trades in 30 minutes
Gas costs unaccounted: 63 trades x ~$0.005 = $0.315 in Polygon fees, not deducted
Low-price markets (13-15c) have widest spreads and highest PnL but also thinnest books - paper mode doesn't simulate queue competition
recommendations: FINAL RECOMMENDATION: Stop paper trading. Go live with tight risk limits.
Live config: $100 capital, one_side, per-order $5, max 3 concurrent positions
Track actual fill rates, adverse selection %, gas costs for 2 weeks minimum
If WR > 60% and PnL > gas costs after 2 weeks -> scale to $500
If WR 50-60% and breakeven -> tune and extend trial
If WR < 50% or negative PnL -> strategy doesn't work live
Expected live return: +2% to -5% per 30-min session (vs +62% paper)
Expected live WR: 60-75% (vs 100% paper)
Expected live adverse fills: 15-25% of trades (vs 0% paper)

session_tracking.description: Every session must be timestamped so we can track performance over time and avoid mixing data across days.
